text: Turn left at the traffic light.
Take the second street on the right.
Cross the road at the crosswalk.
Excuse me, how do I get to the conference center from here? Is it far?
No, it is not far, about a ten minute walk. Go straight down this street for two blocks.
At the second traffic light, turn left onto K Street. Walk past the coffee shop.
So I turn left at the second light, and walk past the coffee shop. Where is the building exactly?
It is the tall glass building on the corner, right across from the park, next to the subway station.
Across from the park, next to the subway station. That is very clear. Thank you so much.
You are welcome. If you reach the museum, you have gone too far. You cannot miss it.
It is across from the park, next to the subway station.

Sure, I can help you get to the conference center. It is not far, about a ten minute walk. From the hotel, go straight down this street for two blocks. At the second traffic light, turn left onto K Street. Walk past the coffee shop and the bank. You will see a small park on the corner. The conference center is the tall glass building right across from the park, next to the subway station. If you reach the museum, you have gone too far, so just turn around. You really cannot miss it.

Excuse me, I am trying to find the National Gallery. Let me see if I understood your directions. So, I go straight down this avenue for three blocks, then I cross the road at the crosswalk near the big landmark. After that, I turn right at the corner and the gallery is the building between the library and the cafe. Is that correct? Thank you so much for your help. I really appreciate it. I think I can find it now.
At the second traffic light, turn left.
Cross the road at the crosswalk.
The hotel is across from the park.
How do I get to the subway station?
The bank is on the corner.
Excuse me, how do I get to the conference center?
Turn left at the traffic light.
It is across from the park, next to the subway station.
